description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Save three recent beginner Wi‑Fi security articles under 8 minutes to your Reading List
tasks.description: Find beginner-friendly Wi‑Fi security posts published from 2023-01-01 onward with estimated reading time of 8 minutes or less, and save the first three matching articles to your Reading List collection.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation bar, click the "Beginner" category.
In the sidebar tag section, click on the "Wi‑Fi Security" tag to filter articles by that topic.
Above the article list, open the sort dropdown and select "Newest first".
In the date filter controls, set the "From" date field to "2023-01-01" and leave the "To" field blank to include all newer posts.
In the reading time filter, set the maximum reading time to "8 minutes".
From the filtered article list, click the bookmark icon on the first three article cards that appear, adding each one to your default Reading List.
In the top navigation bar, click your profile avatar and select "Reading List" from the dropdown to open your saved articles page.

tasks.name: Bookmark the VPN setup guide with more comments from the top two most-commented results
tasks.description: Search for VPN setup guides, restrict to how-to content, sort by most commented, compare the top two results by comment count, and bookmark the one with the higher number of comments into the Practice Later collection.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the search bar in the header, type "VPN setup", and press Enter to run the search.
On the search results page, in the content type filter, select only the "Guides / How‑tos" option.
Open the sort dropdown above the results and choose "Most commented".
Open the first two article cards in the results list in new browser tabs by using Ctrl+click (or your system's equivalent) on each title.
Switch to the browser tab for the first article and scroll until you can see the total number of comments shown near the top of the comments section.
Switch to the tab for the second article, locate its comment count in the same way, and compare it to the first article to decide which one has more comments.
On the article that has the higher comment count, click the "Bookmark" button and, in the dialog that appears, select the "Practice Later" collection to save the article there.

tasks.name: Save the password manager review that recommends the most tools to a Research collection
tasks.description: Search for password manager reviews, open three relevant articles, identify which one’s first recommendation list contains at least 3 tools and the largest number of tools among the three, and add that article to a collection named Research.
steps: From any page on the site, click the search bar in the header.
Type "password manager review" into the search field and press Enter.
In the sidebar "Topic" filter, select both "Tools" and "Account Security" to narrow the results.

Scroll through the first page of results and open the first three article cards whose titles contain the phrase "password manager" or "password managers" in new tabs by Ctrl+clicking (or equivalent) each title.

In the tab for the first article, scroll to the first section that presents recommended tools (for example, a bulleted or numbered list) and count how many distinct tool names are listed in that section.
Repeat the previous step for the second and third article tabs, counting the number of distinct tool names in the first recommendation list of each article.
Compare the three counts and identify the article whose first recommendation list includes at least 3 tools and has the highest number of tools among the three.
Switch to the tab for that article and click the "Add to collection" button located near the bookmark icon.
In the collection dialog, select an existing collection named "Research" or type "Research" to create it, then click the button to save the article to that collection.

tasks.name: Comment on a short, popular phishing article and copy its link
tasks.description: Filter phishing articles to those with reading time of 10 minutes or less and at least 10 upvotes, open the top result sorted by shortest reading time, copy its link using the built-in share tool, and post a text comment on the article.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Threats" category in the main navigation bar.
In the tag filter area on the Threats page, click the "Phishing" tag to show only phishing-related posts.
Open the sort dropdown located above the article list and select "Shortest reading time".
In the sidebar "Reading time" filter, set the maximum reading time value to "10 minutes".
In the "Minimum upvotes" filter input, type "10" to restrict the list to articles with at least 10 upvotes.
Click on the title of the first article in the filtered list (the shortest qualifying article) to open its detail page.
In the share toolbar next to or below the article title, click the "Copy link" button to copy the article URL to your clipboard.
Scroll down to the comment box at the bottom of the article, type a short comment of at least 15 characters, and click the "Post comment" button.

tasks.name: Configure weekly on-site OSINT notifications and disable Malware notifications
tasks.description: Log in with a provided account, open notification settings, set OSINT topic notifications to weekly on-site alerts, and completely disable notifications for the Malware topic, then save the updated settings.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage and click the "Log in" link in the header.
In the login form, enter the username "testuser" and the password "Password123!", then click the "Log in" button.
After logging in, click your profile avatar in the header and select "Settings" from the dropdown menu.
In the settings sidebar, click on the "Notifications" option.
In the notifications topic list, find the row labeled "OSINT" and use its dropdown controls to set notification type to "On-site alerts" and frequency to "Weekly".
In the same list, locate the row labeled "Malware" and change its notification setting to "Disabled" using the available dropdown or toggle.
Scroll to the bottom of the notifications page and click the "Save changes" button to apply the new notification preferences.

tasks.name: Add a 2024 intermediate CTF write-up with 5+ upvotes to a new Weekend Practice collection and mark it In progress
tasks.description: Filter CTF write-ups to intermediate difficulty posts from 2024 with at least 5 upvotes, open the newest matching article, create a Weekend Practice collection, add the article to it, and set its reading status to In progress.
steps: From any page, open the "Categories" menu in the main navigation and choose "CTF & Practice".
In the difficulty filter section on the CTF & Practice page, select only the "Intermediate" checkbox.
In the date range filter controls, set the "From" field to "2024-01-01" and the "To" field to "2024-12-31".
In the "Minimum upvotes" filter input, type "5" so that only posts with 5 or more upvotes are shown.
Open the sort dropdown above the filtered list and select "Newest first".
Click on the title of the first write-up in the filtered list to open its detail page.
Near the article title, click the "Add to collection" button and choose the option to "Create new collection".
In the collection name field of the dialog, type "Weekend Practice" and click the button to create the collection and add the article to it.
Back on the article page, locate the "Reading status" dropdown (for example, showing "Not started") and change its value to "In progress".

tasks.name: Open the first four parts of a Web App Penetration Testing series in separate tabs
tasks.description: Navigate to the series directory, locate the most popular Web Application Security series titled Web App Penetration Testing, and open Parts 1–4 of that series in separate browser tabs, starting to read from the Prerequisites section of Part 1.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage and click the "Series" link in the main navigation bar.
On the Series page, open the "Topic" filter dropdown and select "Web Application Security".
In the sort dropdown on the Series page, choose "Most popular".
From the filtered series list, click the series card titled "Web App Penetration Testing" (or the closest matching title) to open the series overview page.
On the series overview page, scroll to the list of parts and locate Parts 1, 2, 3, and 4 in the sequence.
For Part 1, Ctrl+click (or equivalent) on its title to open it in a new browser tab.
Repeat the same action for Part 2, Part 3, and Part 4 so that each of these parts opens in its own tab.
Switch to the tab for Part 1 and scroll down to the section titled "Prerequisites" to begin reading from that section.

tasks.name: Rate a top Tools article 4 stars and submit detailed feedback
tasks.description: Go to the Tools category, open the highest-rated article, give it a 4-star rating, select at least two predefined feedback reasons, and submit a written comment of at least 20 characters through the feedback form.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Tools" category in the main navigation bar.
In the Tools article list, open the sort dropdown and select "Highest rated".
Click on the title of the first article in the sorted list to open its detail page.
Scroll to the bottom of the article until you reach the "Was this helpful?" or "Feedback" section.
In the star rating widget, click on the 4th star to assign a 4-out-of-5 rating to the article.
In the multiple-choice feedback options, select at least two reasons that explain your rating (for example, "Clear but missing examples" and "Too short").
In the optional text comment field, type a brief feedback message of at least 20 characters, then click the "Submit feedback" button.
